steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Extract the circle's center and radius
steps.content:
The equation $(x+6)^2 + (y-17)^2 = 144$ is in standard form.
Center: $(-6, 17)$
Radius: $\sqrt{144} = 12$

steps.title: Determine which x-coordinate is on the circle
steps.content:
For a point $(a, b)$ on the circle, $(a+6)^2 + (b-17)^2 = 144$.
Rearranging: $(b-17)^2 = 144 - (a+6)^2$.
For $b$ to be real, we need $(a+6)^2 \leq 144$.

Backsolving by testing the candidate ($a = -17$):
$(a+6)^2 = (-17+6)^2 = (-11)^2 = 121 \leq 144$ ✓

Testing the others:
  • $-19$: $(-19+6)^2 = (-13)^2 = 169 \gt 144$ — Fails
  • $12$: $(12+6)^2 = 18^2 = 324 \gt 144$ — Fails
  • $17$: $(17+6)^2 = 23^2 = 529 \gt 144$ — Fails
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
